hero_subtitle: SFTY connects multiple registry sources and screening providers into a single KYB workflow to streamline VASP onboarding with clear audit trails and structured risk reviews.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling

problem_title: Onboarding delays caused by disconnected registry data
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to create a unified view when company registry checks and KYB data remain separated across multiple providers. This leads to slower onboarding, manual reconciliation efforts, and weaker audit trails for corporate onboarding decisions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY aggregates company registry data, sanctions and PEP screening outputs, and KYC/KYB provider results into a unified onboarding workflow with risk-based routing and audit-ready evidence for compliance teams.
workflow_title: From company checks to onboarding approval
workflow_description: SFTY connects registry data, KYC and KYB provider outputs through risk-based workflows, case reviews, decisions and audit-ready onboarding evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One onboarding layer

why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs in connecting registry data, preserving audit-ready evidence, and enabling bank due diligence within governed, enterprise-grade compliance workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for KYB onboarding workflows?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects KYC, KYB and registry checks into structured onboarding workflows with risk-based routing, case management and audit-ready evidence for regulated digital asset businesses like VASPs.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY orchestrate company registry data?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates data from multiple registry sources, sanctions, PEP and adverse media screens into a connected KYB workflow to enable consistent, auditable corporate client risk review.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and supporting documents as part of onboarding evidence packages to support compliance review and audit readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing KYB and KYC providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing KYC and KYB providers, connecting their outputs into one compliance operating layer without replacing them.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace onboarding or registry providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY orchestrates inputs from onboarding and registry providers rather than replacing them, enabling consistent workflows and audit trails across multiple data sources.
final_title: Structure company registry data into one workflow
final_description: Review how SFTY connects registry sources, sanctions and PEP screening into audit-ready KYB onboarding cases and evidence.
